parameters.query: SELECT key, content FROM soul WHERE key LIKE 'wedding.%' ORDER BY key
SELECT key, content FROM agents WHERE key LIKE 'wedding.%' ORDER BY key
SELECT name, display_name, timezone, preferences, context FROM user_profiles WHERE user_id = 'telegram:{{ $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.from.id }}' LIMIT 1
SELECT role, content FROM conversations WHERE session_id = 'telegram:{{ $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.chat.id }}' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 20

INSERT INTO conversations (session_id, user_id, role, content) VALUES ('{{ $json.sessionId }}', '{{ $json.userId }}', 'user', '{{ $json.userMessage.replace(/'/g, "''") }}'); INSERT INTO conversations (session_id, user_id, role, content) VALUES ('{{ $json.sessionId }}', '{{ $json.userId }}', 'assistant', '{{ $json.assistantMessage.replace(/'/g, "''") }}');
INSERT INTO memory_daily (date, content, role, user_id) VALUES (CURRENT_DATE, '{{ ($json.userMessage + ' → ' + $json.assistantMessage).replace(/'/g, "''") }}', 'interaction', '{{ $json.userId }}')

parameters.description: Search long-term memory. Input: JSON with search_query or query.
Save important information to long-term memory. Input: JSON with content, category, and importance.

Manage the wedding Wekan board. Input: a structured JSON payload for board/list/card/comment/checklist actions. For mutations, call it once without confirmed=true to preview, then call it again with the same payload and confirmed=true after the user approves.
List workflows or fetch workflow details from this local openclaw instance. Requires N8N_API_KEY in the runtime environment.

Set a timed reminder that sends a Telegram message at a specific time. Use when user says 'remind me at...', 'remind me in X minutes/hours'. Input: time as ISO 8601 (e.g. 2026-03-01T10:00:00+01:00) and message text.

Builds new n8n workflows for general automations and integrations. Use when the user explicitly wants to automate something or build an integration workflow. NOT for MCP servers — use mcp_builder for that. NOT as a fallback when other tools fail. Input: JSON with 'task' (description of the automation to build).

parameters.name: mcp_builder
parameters.description: Builds a new MCP Server workflow in n8n. ALWAYS use this when the user wants to build an MCP server, MCP tool, or API integration as MCP. NEVER use WorkflowBuilder for MCP. On error: report the error, do NOT fall back to WorkflowBuilder. Input: JSON with 'task' (description of what the server should do). After build: user must deactivate + reactivate the workflow in n8n UI (webhook registration bug).

Calls a tool on an MCP server. Use when the user asks something that an available MCP server can answer (weather, book lookup, etc.). Required parameters: mcp_url (server URL), tool_name (tool to call), arguments (JSON object with parameters). Check mcp_registry table for available servers and their tools.
